package handler

import (
	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
	"net/http"
)

// Positive scenario for service_type_outside_service_package: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Concrete use-case services declared outside `service` or `services` packages in layered web-service repos.
type CaseServiceTypeOutsideServicePackageDTO struct {
	ID       string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
	TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
	Status   string `json:"status" binding:"required"`
	Amount   int    `json:"amount"`
}

type CaseServiceTypeOutsideServicePackageModel struct {
	ID       string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
	TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
	Status   string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
}

type CaseServiceTypeOutsideServicePackageRepository struct {
	db *gorm.DB
}

type CaseServiceTypeOutsideServicePackageService struct {
	repo *CaseServiceTypeOutsideServicePackageRepository
}

func (h *CaseServiceTypeOutsideServicePackageService) HandleCreate(c *gin.Context) {
	var dto CaseServiceTypeOutsideServicePackageDTO
	if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&dto); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}

	model := CaseServiceTypeOutsideServicePackageModel{
		ID:       dto.ID,
		TenantID: dto.TenantID,
		Status:   dto.Status,
	}

	if err := h.repo.db.Create(&model).Error; err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": "failed to create"})
		return
	}

	c.JSON(http.StatusCreated, gin.H{"id": model.ID, "status": model.Status})
}
